entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arientinum, the host plant studied for resistance against fungal pathogens

entities.name: DEFENSIN GENES
entities.description: A family of genes encoding antimicrobial peptides identified in chickpea

entities.name: DEFENSIN-LIKE GENES
entities.description: A family of genes similar to defensins identified in chickpea

entities.name: INNATE IMMUNITY
entities.description: The plant's natural ability to provide defense against biotic stresses

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Stresses caused by living organisms such as fungal pathogens

entities.name: GENE DUPLICATION
entities.description: The process by which gene clusters evolved in the chickpea genome

entities.name: FUNGAL BIOASSAYS
entities.description: Experimental assays used to test the defense responses of genes against fungi

entities.name: DEFENSE
entities.description: The biological response of the plant to protect itself against fungal pathogens

entities.name: FUSARIUM OXYSPORUM F. SP. CICERIS
entities.description: A hemi-biotrophic fungal pathogen affecting chickpea

entities.name: RHIZOCTONIA BATATICOLA
entities.description: A necrotrophic fungal pathogen that causes dry root rot in chickpea

entities.name: DRY ROOT ROT
entities.description: A disease in chickpea caused by the fungus Rhizoctonia bataticola

entities.name: MOLECULAR DOCKING
entities.description: Computational studies used to reveal interactions between proteins and membrane components

entities.name: PHOSPHATIDYLSERINE
entities.description: A fungal plasma membrane component that interacts with CaDEFs

entities.name: GLUCOSYLCERAMIDE
entities.description: A fungal plasma membrane component that interacts with CaDEFs

entities.name: HOST-PLANT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the chickpea plant to resist infection by pathogens
